user_query:
I’m planning my Q2 weekly routine to better balance writing my vanilla JavaScript blog with fitness, while keeping evenings free for family. Please use the local files under data/, drafts/, and docs/ to propose a concrete plan and update my docs and comms accordingly.

Inputs (relative to workspace):
- data/commitments.csv
- data/gym_options.csv
- data/posts.json
- drafts/ (count the .md files inside)
- docs/q2_goals.md (update this file in place)

Requirements:
1) Build a weekly routine that:
   - Chooses a single gym option from data/gym_options.csv that avoids evenings (no sessions that start at or after 18:00), provides at least 3 sessions per week, and does not overlap with any entries in data/commitments.csv (assume those commitments recur weekly). When evaluating conflicts, treat commitment times as fixed and gym sessions as the listed start–end times; you don’t need to include commute buffers in the output, but do consider them when deciding if a gym is viable.
   - Among the gyms that satisfy those constraints, select the one with the smallest commute_minutes_each_way; break ties by lower cost_per_month.
   - Schedules exactly two writing blocks per week that each finish by 10:00 (local time), and do not overlap with commitments or the selected gym sessions.
   - Picks a weekly publish time aligned to when readers are most engaged: compute the top average engagement hour-of-day from data/posts.json by flooring each post’s published_at to the hour and averaging engagement_score per hour; set the publish_time hour to this top hour and choose a weekday that doesn’t overlap with the selected gym sessions or commitments.
   - Incorporates the drafts backlog: count how many .md files are in drafts/ and include that count in your outputs.

2) Deliverables:
   a) Write output/weekly_routine.json with this structure:
      {
        "selected_gym": "<string exactly matching a gym name from data/gym_options.csv>",
        "gym_sessions": [ {"day": "<Monday|Tuesday|...>", "start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM"}, ... ],
        "writing_blocks": [ {"day": "<Monday|Tuesday|...>", "start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM"}, {"day": "...", "start": "HH:MM", "end": "HH:MM"} ],
        "publish_time": {"day": "<Monday|Tuesday|...>", "hour": <integer 0-23>},
        "summary": {"top_engagement_hour": <integer 0-23>, "draft_count": <integer>}
      }
      - Ensure: at least 3 gym_sessions; writing_blocks finish by 10:00; publish_time.hour equals summary.top_engagement_hour; publish_time does not overlap with gym sessions or commitments.
   b) Edit docs/q2_goals.md in place: replace the text between the markers BEGIN_WEEKLY_ROUTINE and END_WEEKLY_ROUTINE with a brief paragraph (3–6 sentences) summarizing the selected gym plan, the number of weekly gym sessions, the two morning writing blocks, the chosen weekly publish day/time, and a one-sentence rationale referencing the top engagement hour and keeping evenings free.
   c) Draft an email-style message to subscribers at output/newsletter_update.md that:
      - Starts with a single-line subject in the form: "Subject: <your subject line>".
      - Includes 2–3 short paragraphs announcing the new weekly posting time (specify weekday and hour), what to expect (vanilla JavaScript-focused content), and a personal note about prioritizing mornings and keeping evenings free.
      - Includes a final line starting with "P.S." that mentions how many drafts are currently in progress (use the count from drafts/).

Notes:
- Treat time strings as 24-hour HH:MM with no timezone conversions.
- Do not invent external data; derive the engagement hour from data/posts.json and the draft count from drafts/.
- Keep all new artifacts under the specified output/ path, and edit only the marked section in docs/q2_goals.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
